novel_features.name: The God cup recommender
novel_features.description: Integrates your shelf, brew history, Coffee Review scores, Hoffmann/Hedrick coverage, and champion recipes to produce one brew-now pick and one buy-next pick.
novel_features.rationale: Five-table local join (beans + brews + reviews + youtube_reviews + champion_recipes) producing a deterministic weighted score — only possible because every signal source is in one local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this as the headline 'what should I do next' question; integrates every signal source into one answer.

novel_features.name: Cross-roaster FTS search
novel_features.description: Search every elite specialty roaster's catalog at once — Ethiopian naturals across the curated shelf in one query.
novel_features.rationale: Requires synced cross-roaster corpus + FTS5 index that no single roaster's site can provide.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs a cross-source, structured product query rather than visiting many storefronts.

novel_features.name: Persisted query diff feed
novel_features.description: Saved search emits only new matches on each sync — silent when nothing changed, cron-safe.
novel_features.rationale: Requires sync-anchored diff over cross-roaster corpus.
novel_features.example: coffee-goat watch save bermudez "diego bermudez gesha" --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when a user wants to be alerted the moment a specific producer or origin appears anywhere.

novel_features.name: Closest twin across roasters
novel_features.description: Given a bean you loved, find the closest current match across the curated shelf via origin/varietal/process/altitude/descriptor similarity.
novel_features.rationale: Requires the entire cross-roaster corpus and a similarity engine over structured attributes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to replace a sold-out favorite without manually scanning every other roaster.

novel_features.name: Multi-bean compare
novel_features.description: Side-by-side bean table with origin/process/altitude/$/oz/score deltas; works across roasters.
novel_features.rationale: Requires the cross-roaster table + Coffee Review join.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when choosing between candidates from different roasters with structured deltas.

novel_features.name: Producer tracking across roasters
novel_features.description: Track a producer or farm (Diego Bermudez, Wush Wush, El Paraiso) across every roaster sync — multi-year lineage view.
novel_features.rationale: Requires producer field extraction from body_html across roasters + cross-roaster join.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to compare different roasters' interpretations of one producer or to track lot evolution.

novel_features.name: FX-normalized landed cost
novel_features.description: Live ECB FX rates normalize $/oz across continents; curated shipping table adds ship-to landed cost.
novel_features.rationale: Requires daily FX fetch + per-roaster shipping reference that no roaster site exposes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this before ordering from JP/DK/UK/DE roasters to compare true landed cost.

novel_features.name: Bayesian dial-in recommender
novel_features.description: Suggest grind/dose/yield/time for a new bean using your brew history of similar beans (origin/process/varietal/altitude clusters).
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining your personal brew log against the global product corpus.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to start with a confidence-banded recipe instead of burning beans on cold-start dial-ins.

novel_features.name: Cellar with freshness windows
novel_features.description: What's on your shelf today, sorted by freshness with per-method peak windows (espresso 8–21d, filter 5–28d).
novel_features.rationale: Requires roast-date + per-method freshness curve overlay that incumbents don't compute.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to answer 'what should I open next' with hard freshness rules.

novel_features.name: Recommend what to drink next
novel_features.description: Joins shelf freshness × dial-in confidence × user palate signature to pick one bag from your shelf for tomorrow morning.
novel_features.rationale: Requires the local join across beans + brews + freshness model that no single API answers.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when the household agent needs one deterministic 'brew this' answer at dawn.

novel_features.name: Brew-rating drift diagnostic
novel_features.description: Detect grinder/water/technique drift via fixed-effects OLS on rating-vs-day across multiple beans; partitions variance by method.
novel_features.rationale: Requires longitudinal brew log + statistical model.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when ratings slide across unrelated beans — the CLI tells you it's your hardware, not the coffee.

novel_features.name: Refill plan with ranked replacements
novel_features.description: Combines consumption rate from your brews + shelf depletion + cross-roaster twin similarity to recommend 3 replacement picks.
novel_features.rationale: Requires personal brew-log analytics joined with cross-roaster product corpus.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to never run out of coffee and always restock toward your taste profile.

novel_features.name: Blind cupping with palate calibration
novel_features.description: Blind cupping session with hashed cup IDs; reveal at end and report Spearman correlation against Coffee Review's scores.
novel_features.rationale: Requires the SCA cupping form + synced Coffee Review corpus.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to quantify how your palate maps to published scores over time.

novel_features.name: Personal palate map
novel_features.description: Aggregate your 8+ rated brews into an origin/process/varietal weight signature, then rank current shelf and restock candidates by signature match.
novel_features.rationale: Per-user descriptor signature learned from `brews ⋈ roaster_products`; no incumbent computes per-user palate weights.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to see what flavor profile you actually prefer (vs what you claim), and rank everything by it.

novel_features.name: Bag lifecycle
novel_features.description: For one bag, plot daily ratings vs days-since-roast and call peak / decline / dead.
novel_features.rationale: Personal rating curve overlaid on freshness model; runs on the user's local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to decide whether a struggling shot is the bag dying or your technique slipping.

novel_features.name: Friend-pick (palate sharing)
novel_features.description: Recommend a bag for a friend whose palate profile you've imported — pick from your shelf or current market, ranked against their descriptor signature instead of yours.
novel_features.rationale: Joins imported friend `palate_profile` with cross-roaster corpus + your brews; uses portable palate-export/import for sharing.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs to recommend a gift bag or birthday-coffee pick that fits someone else's documented palate.

novel_features.name: SCA Flavor Wheel mapping
novel_features.description: Maps your brew ratings onto the official SCA Coffee Tasters' Flavor Wheel hierarchy (fruity → berry → blackberry, floral → tea-like → black tea, etc.) and shows which sections you actually prefer.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates `brews.rating × roaster_products.descriptors` against a curated SCA-wheel taxonomy (`// pp:novel-static-reference`); complements palate-map (which works by origin/process/varietal).
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when the user wants to get clearer on what flavor families they actually like vs claim to like, against the canonical specialty-coffee taxonomy.

novel_features.name: Creator review lookup
novel_features.description: Find every Hoffmann or Hedrick clip that mentions a bean or roaster, with transcript excerpt and timestamp.
novel_features.rationale: Requires the local `youtube_reviews` table populated via youtube-pp-cli; no incumbent indexes creators against bags.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when the user remembers Hoffmann or Hedrick talking about a bag and wants the exact clip in 30 seconds, not a 20-minute rewatch.

novel_features.name: Transcript search
novel_features.description: Local FTS5 search across all synced Hoffmann + Hedrick transcripts with timestamp + video link.
novel_features.rationale: FTS over `youtube_reviews(transcript_excerpt, video_title)`; impossible on YouTube's native UI.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs the exact clip where a creator said X.

novel_features.name: Personal brew log + cellar
novel_features.description: Brew log (log/list/show/delete) and local cellar (cellar add/list/show/update/remove) — the input feed for every personal-history command.
novel_features.rationale: Local SQLite-backed log; required by every analytics command and the cupping-to-brew bridge.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to start building the personal-history signal that powers dial-in, drift, palate-map, predict-rating, and whats-next.

novel_features.name: Curated V60 recipe library
novel_features.description: Hardcoded library of 10 canonical V60 recipes (Hoffmann 2018/2023, Tetsu 4:6 light/dark, Lance Pulse, WBrC 2020-2024 champions) with structured 5-tuple + technique markdown.
novel_features.rationale: Static-reference data marked pp:novel-static-reference; no API has a structured cross-creator champion-recipe corpus.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to bridge from theoretical champion routines to your specific bean — match scores by bean features, apply scales dose+water, --log pre-fills a brews row.

novel_features.name: Coffee spend + cost-per-cup
novel_features.description: Dose-grams attribution (dose_g / bag_size_g × bag_price). YTD + projection range (linear + trailing-3-mo × 12). Pivot --by month|roaster|bag|method. --include-shipping uses fx's curated table.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining brews × beans × roaster_products with fx for currency. Skip-and-warn on missing data.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to answer 'am I overspending vs last quarter?' or 'which bag was actually expensive per-cup?' — money axis nothing else touches.

novel_features.name: Origin coverage + gap-filler
novel_features.description: Country-primary coverage with tiered depth labels (unexplored/tasted/explored/deep). Synced corpus default; --world adds curated specialty origins. --fill suggests bags inline; `coffee-map fill` ranks across all gaps by CR score + process tiebreak.
novel_features.rationale: Requires curated origin-string→country canonicalization (pp:novel-static-reference) plus brew-history aggregation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to discover gaps in your origin coverage ('you've never had a Burundi') and get a concrete bag to close each gap.

novel_features.name: K-NN twin prediction + back-test
novel_features.description: Predicts YOUR rating for a (bean, method) pair via K=5 nearest twins from your brew log. Subcommand split: `predict-rating [bean]` and `predict-rating cellar` write to predicted_ratings; `predict-rating calibration` is mcp:read-only and reports MAE+bias sliced by method/origin/process.
novel_features.rationale: Reuses twin similarity scoring against logged brews; persists predictions for honest ex-ante back-test (scored against first brew after prediction timestamp).
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this before buying a new bag to estimate how you'll rate it, and run calibration periodically to see if the model over-predicts naturals or under-predicts darks.

novel_features.name: SCA cupping sessions
novel_features.description: Full 10-attribute SCA form, multi-cupper sessions (auto-creates palate_profiles), open default + --blind slot labels. 7 verbs: start/score/finalize/abandon/show/list/log. Finalize bridges cupper=self scores to the brews table as method='cupping'.
novel_features.rationale: 3 new tables (cupping_sessions, cupping_session_beans, cupping_scores) with auto-migration; bridge ensures cupping scores enrich every personal-history analytic.
novel_features.example: coffee-goat cupping start --name "Sat AM" --bean sey/banko-gotiti --bean april/ethiopia-natural
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to run a structured tasting (solo or multi-cupper, open or blind) and have the scores flow into drift/palate-map/predict-rating automatically.

novel_features_built.name: The God cup recommender
novel_features_built.description: Integrates your shelf, brew history, Coffee Review scores, Hoffmann/Hedrick coverage, and champion recipes to produce one brew-now pick and one buy-next pick.
novel_features_built.rationale: Five-table local join (beans + brews + reviews + youtube_reviews + champion_recipes) producing a deterministic weighted score — only possible because every signal source is in one local store.
novel_features_built.why_it_matters: Pick this as the headline 'what should I do next' question; integrates every signal source into one answer.

narrative.headline: The third-wave coffee terminal — every elite roaster, every YouTube creator review, your brews, and the God cup.
narrative.value_prop: coffee-goat aggregates the global specialty-coffee shelf via Shopify-backed roaster storefronts, Coffee Review scores, and James Hoffmann + Lance Hedrick YouTube transcripts (via the youtube-pp-cli sibling) into one no-auth local SQLite corpus — then joins it with your personal brew log. Cross-roaster search, restock watch, closest-twin replacement, creator-clip lookup, SCA flavor-wheel palate mapping, friend-pick recommendations, and the eponymous `god-cup` recommender that integrates shelf freshness + brew history + Coffee Review + creator coverage into one brew-now and one buy-next pick. (Champion recipes, cafe finder, drift diagnostics, and 11 other commands documented in the absorb manifest are Phase 3 follow-up.)
narrative.auth_narrative: No API key required. All shipped sources are no-auth: roaster storefronts via public /products.json, Coffee Review WP REST, and YouTube transcripts via the youtube-pp-cli sibling (timedtext endpoint). Optional: `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` enables bean-bag photo scanning if/when the scan command is wired up. youtube-pp-cli must be on PATH for creator features; run `coffee-goat doctor` to verify.

quickstart.comment: Confirm youtube-pp-cli is on PATH and the representative roaster (Onyx) is reachable
Pull catalogs from Shopify roasters, Coffee Review, and the Hoffmann + Hedrick YouTube channels into the local store
Cross-roaster origin/process search — every Ethiopian natural under $25 ($25.00 = 2500 cents) across the global shelf
Find the closest current match across roasters for a bean you loved (use any synced roaster_products handle)
Show every Hoffmann/Hedrick clip mentioning Onyx with transcript excerpts
Integrates shelf + brews + Coffee Review + creator transcripts into one brew-now and one buy-next pick (works against fixture data; cellar/brew CLI is Phase 3 follow-up)

troubleshoots.symptom: doctor says "youtube-pp-cli not found on PATH"
troubleshoots.fix: Install youtube-pp-cli from the printing-press public library, then rerun `coffee-goat doctor`. Creator features (creator-review, god-cup) are degraded but not broken without it — sync just skips the YouTube source with a warning.

troubleshoots.symptom: sync returns 0 products for a roaster
troubleshoots.fix: Run `coffee-goat sync --source shopify --roaster <slug>` to scope to one roaster; the registry handles known corrections for Leaves and Friedhats (their canonical hostnames).
Confirm `coffee-goat sync` exited 0 and reported items for at least one source. Then try a broader query like `coffee-goat search ethiopia` to rule out FTS5 tokenizer edge cases.

troubleshoots.symptom: twin returns very low similarity scores
troubleshoots.fix: Synced descriptor coverage varies by roaster. Tag and origin fields drive most of the similarity; sparse descriptor coverage on a roaster will reduce twin precision for its bags.

troubleshoots.symptom: creator-review returns no clips for a bag you remember being mentioned
troubleshoots.fix: Bean-mention extraction is regex match against the registered roaster slug/name set. If the creator referred to the roaster by a non-canonical name, the regex misses it. Re-run `coffee-goat sync --source youtube` after updating registry names.

troubleshoots.symptom: flavor-wheel shows mostly empty wheel sections
troubleshoots.fix: flavor-wheel needs at least 8 rated brews to populate the SCA wheel meaningfully. Personal brew-log CLI (`brews log`) is Phase 3 follow-up; for now seed via SQL: `INSERT INTO brews(bean_id, rating, descriptors_json) VALUES (...)`.

Run `coffee-goat friend-pick palate-export <your-name> --out you.json` on your machine and have your friend run the same; exchange JSONs and import via `coffee-goat friend-pick palate-import friend.json`.

narrative.when_to_use: Use coffee-goat when an agent needs to compare or recommend specialty coffee across many roasters at once, when answering 'what should I drink next' or 'what should I buy next' from a user's logged cellar, when finding YouTube creator coverage of a specific bag in seconds instead of rewatching a 20-minute video, when matching champion recipes to currently-buyable beans, when mapping a user's palate onto the SCA flavor wheel, when picking a gift bag for a friend whose palate profile is imported, when finding cafes near a location, or when diagnosing whether a user's recent low ratings are bean quality or equipment drift. Do NOT use it for ordering — coffee-goat is read-only across upstream sources. Do NOT use it to find which beans a cafe serves — no public no-auth source links cafes to beans.

recipes.title: The God cup — one brew, one buy
recipes.explanation: The eponymous command. Integrates shelf freshness, brew history, Coffee Review scores, and creator coverage into one brew-now pick and one buy-next pick. The --select narrows to the agent-relevant fields when the response is deep.

recipes.title: Did Hoffmann or Hedrick ever review this bag
recipes.explanation: Returns every clip that mentions the bag, with timestamp and a snippet — answers in 30 seconds what would otherwise be a 20-minute rewatch.

recipes.title: Restock alert across roasters
recipes.explanation: Save a cross-roaster query; on subsequent runs `coffee-goat watch run kenyas` emits only new matches since last sync.

recipes.title: Map your palate onto the SCA Flavor Wheel
recipes.explanation: Aggregates your rated brews against the official SCA Coffee Tasters Flavor Wheel hierarchy — shows which flavor families you actually like.

recipes.title: Recommend a bag for a friend whose palate you have
recipes.explanation: Import a friend palate via `coffee-goat friend-pick palate-import friend.json`; pick recommends bags matching their descriptor signature, not yours.

trigger_phrases: find a bean like the one I loved
what flavors do I actually like
recommend a bag for my friend
